lesson.menu_desc: Ordering at a restaurant in Paris -- I'd like / Could I have + Can you recommend -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Eating Out Like a Local -- Ordering & Food
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gabriela Pires | Aula 14 -- Eating Out Like a Local | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to our restaurant. Thank you for your reservation. Tonight we have a few specials for you. As an appetizer, we have a fresh tomato soup. For the main course, the chef recommends the chicken with rice, or a spicy fish from the coast. If you like meat, our steak is very popular; you can have it well-done or medium. For dessert, we have a chocolate cake. And do not worry about the check; you can pay at the table when you finish. If you need anything, just call the waiter. Enjoy your evening, and have a wonderful meal.

Tonight I went to a restaurant in Paris with my family, and I ordered everything in English. First, I told the host we had a reservation for a table for two. The waiter was very kind. I asked him, can you recommend something? He recommended the spicy fish, but I am not good with spicy food, so I said, I would like the chicken, please. For my mom, I ordered a steak, well-done. As an appetizer, we had soup, and for dessert, a chocolate cake. At the end, I asked, could I have the check, please? I even left a small tip for the waiter. I was a little nervous, but I ordered our whole dinner in English, and I felt so proud.

extra_audio.text: First, you tell the host you have a reservation. Next, the waiter brings the menu and you ask, can you recommend something? After that, you order your appetizer and your main course. Then, you eat your meal and you order a dessert. Finally, you ask for the check and you leave a small tip.
